title: Willughbeia auriculata
intro: Willughbeia auriculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the golden ear vine. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Willughbeia auriculata include alkaloids, particularly willughbeine and willughbeinol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include extracts and infusions, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
